task_completion.description: Verify that the target image with white borders is loaded into GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image is displayed in the GIMP workspace, and a distinct, uniform white border is visible around the main photo content.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Image' menu is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Image' menu is dropped down from the top menu bar in the GIMP application.

task_completion.description: Verify that the autocrop feature is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Autocrop Image' (in GIMP 2.8) or 'Crop to Content' (in GIMP 2.10+) option is visible and highlighted or clicked in the Image menu.

task_completion.description: Verify that the white borders are removed from the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the GIMP workspace no longer has white borders, and the canvas boundaries tightly wrap the main photo content.

task_completion.description: Verify that the modified image is saved or exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog is open, the 'File' menu shows 'Overwrite [filename]' or 'Export As...' being selected, or there is evidence of the file being saved before closing.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the GIMP GUI to crop the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the GIMP interface rather than typing terminal commands like ImageMagick's 'convert' or 'mogrify' to crop the image.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the automatic crop feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent selects 'Autocrop Image' or 'Crop to Content' from the menu, rather than manually drawing a rectangle with the Crop tool from the toolbox.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent modified the provided image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being edited has the 50-pixel white border initially, rather than the agent downloading or opening a pre-cropped version of the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires removing a 50-pixel white border from an image using GIMP. The agent must use GIMP's autocrop feature, accessed via 'Image -> Autocrop Image' (GIMP 2.8) or 'Image -> Crop to Content' (GIMP 2.10+). The original image has dimensions of 700x500 or 700x438 (including the white border). After autocropping, the white border should be completely removed, resulting in an area reduction of approximately 31-34%, leaving only the main photo content. The agent must save or overwrite the file for the verifier to detect the changes.
